title: Adenogramma sylvatica
taxonomy: Adenogramma sylvatica is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as daisies or sunflowers. It is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The classification of Adenogramma sylvatica is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Adenogramma, species - A. sylvatica. The specific classification allows for a clear understanding of its evolutionary relationships within the plant kingdom. Adenogramma sylvatica is an example of how a plant's classification can be used to inform its conservation and management.

intro: Adenogramma sylvatica, also known as the "sylvatic adenogramma," is a plant species belonging to the family Asteraceae, traditionally used in folk medicine. The health benefits of Adenogramma sylvatica include its anti-inflammatory, antimalarial, and antimicrobial properties, which have been utilized to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, Adenogramma sylvatica is used to make herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
